critical.mandate: Target WCAG level must be declared before testing begins
Automated checks must cover ALL identified pages and components

step.title: Scope
step.action: Identify which pages and components to test
Declare target WCAG level: A, AA, or AAA
Document user personas including users with disabilities

step.title: Automated Checks
step.action: Load testing/knowledge/accessibility/axe-core-patterns.md
Design axe-core or pa11y integration for each target page
Define automated test scenarios covering all identified components
Configure rule sets matching the declared WCAG level

step.title: Manual Test Plan
step.action: Define keyboard navigation testing procedure for all interactive elements
Define screen reader testing procedure (VoiceOver for macOS/iOS, NVDA for Windows)
Define color contrast verification steps (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
Document focus order expectations for each page

step.title: ARIA Audit
step.action: Review ARIA roles and labels for correctness
Verify focus management on modal dialogs, dropdowns, and dynamic content
Check live regions (aria-live) for dynamic content updates
Validate landmark regions (nav, main, aside, footer)

step.title: Remediation Priorities
step.action: Categorize findings by impact level:
Critical — blocks access entirely (no keyboard nav, missing alt text on functional images)
High — significantly degrades experience (poor focus indicators, missing form labels)
Medium — inconvenient but workaround exists (suboptimal heading hierarchy)
Low — enhancement opportunity (decorative improvements)
Provide at least one remediation recommendation per critical finding

step.title: Generate Report
step.action: Generate accessibility report with findings, priorities, and remediation steps
